capture_method: Manual local replay against the committed boundary fixture and the extracted public-draft skill contract.

observed_output_sections.heading: queue states and actions
observed_output_sections.content: The replay answer treats direct card actions and their state matrix as the main work, while acknowledging that label cleanup can happen afterward.

observed_output_sections.heading: card interaction contract
observed_output_sections.content: The answer keeps the boundary on queue-card design by defining card tap versus inline mutation responsibilities.

observed_output_sections.heading: refresh and side-effect chain
observed_output_sections.content: The replay answer makes the refresh chain explicit, which is the highest-value reusable element in this boundary case.

observed_output_sections.heading: files or components to change first
observed_output_sections.content: The answer points to queue-card and mutation-flow owners first instead of starting with text strings, which confirms the expected skill choice.

notes: Boundary replay confirming that interaction friction in a queue should route to the queue-action skill even when the prompt also mentions wordy labels.
